meta.title: Korobkov Art Studio
meta.description: Contemporary Ukrainian art studio. Original paintings, murals, and installations by Mykhailo Korobkov & Olha Korobkova.

series.podilia_desc: Ancient ornaments of traditional Podil carpets intertwined with the human figure. History meets modernity.
series.destruction_desc: Every event of our lives leaves its imprint. With the war, the negative imprints became much more abundant.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.no_results: No works found in this category.

about.title: About
about.bio: Born in 1985 in Crimea, Ukraine. Lives and works in Kyiv. Graduated from the Crimean Art College named after Samokish (painting) and the Kharkiv State Academy of Design and Arts (monumental painting).
about.statement_podilia: The composition of the Podilia series was based on the ancient ornaments of traditional Podil carpets, which we combine with the human figure. We convey meaning not only through images but also through technique, combining oil painting with modern materials such as acrylic aerosol paints. There is great beauty in the female figure, each shape is imbued with exceptional aesthetics and character. We represent real modern people who've been living in war and all of them are from Podillia.
about.statement_destruction: Every event of our lives leaves its imprint, its noticeable trace. Sadly, our present shows us that the negative imprints became much more abundant. With the beginning of the war not only Russian missiles have broken into our reality, but also crippled fates, countless tears of grief, and the absence of home for many Ukrainian people. But despite all of this I am trying to look into the future with optimism, to build a bright reality for my children and a successful future for our country.
about.education_1: Crimean Art College named after Samokish (painter)
about.education_2: Kharkiv Academy of Design and Art (monumental painting)

partners.title: Partners
partners.subtitle: Collaborate with Korobkov Art Studio
partners.why_text: Korobkov Art Studio brings a unique voice in contemporary Ukrainian art — blending ancient Podil carpet ornaments with modern techniques, creating works that bridge history and present day. Our art has been exhibited in London, Krakow, Cordoba, Wroclaw, Istanbul, and Kyiv.
partners.type_gallery_desc: Host a solo or group exhibition featuring our paintings, murals, and installations.
partners.type_mural_desc: Commission a large-scale mural for your space — public, commercial, or private.
partners.type_curatorial_desc: Include our works in curatorial projects exploring Ukrainian contemporary art, identity, and war.
partners.type_corporate_desc: Acquire original artworks for corporate collections and office spaces.
